name: White Chocolate Cream Filling
intro: This is an updated version of the cream that filled my infamous Organic Vegan Twinkies in 2007. The cream is so good though, that I had to find other reasons to use it. This cream still fills Twinkies, as well as the update to Éclairs (page 119). It is a component in Nesselrode Pie (page 160), makes a wonderful cake filling, and is lovely in parfaits layered with fruit, nuts, and chopped chocolate.

ingredients.item: mild tasting extra-virgin olive oil or coconut oil
maple syrup, Grade B or dark amber, or agave syrup
/ 50 grams organic granulated sugar, lightly ground in a blender
/ 142 grams vegan white chocolate, melted and kept warm in a water bath (see Resources page 290)
plus 1 tablespoon / 75 ml almond milk or soy milk

method: Crumble the tofu into a food processor. Add the oil, maple syrup or agave, and salt and process for 1 minute. Clean the sides of the bowl with a silicone spatula. Add the sugar and the vanilla and almond extracts and process for 1 minute.
Add the melted white chocolate. Pulse a few times to incorporate and process for 1 minute. Keep the tofu purée in the food processor while you cook the agar-arrowroot slurry.

Pour the almond milk into a small saucepan. Sprinkle the agar onto the milk and set aside for 1 minute. Add the arrowroot and whisk until the arrowroot is completely dissolved. Cook the slurry to a boil over medium-low heat, whisking almost constantly. The slurry will be so thick and gummy that the boil will look like sputtering lava. As soon as the sputtering and bubbling reaches the center, remove the saucepan from the heat.
Add the slurry to the food processor and pulse to incorporate. Process for 1 minute until the cream is smooth.
